page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-64 is an x86_64 architecture server instance featuring 64 dedicated virtual CPUs and 248.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides approximately 3.88 GB of RAM per core, establishing a balanced resource allocation profile.

This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage options. Additionally, the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced memory-to-core ratio, the c4d-standard-64 is designed for workloads requiring consistent compute performance without the need for local NVMe drives or hardware acceleration. Suitable applications include web serving, application servers, containerized microservices, and moderate database workloads. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy tasks that rely on external storage systems.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-64 is an x86_64 architecture server instance configured with 64 dedicated virtual CPUs and 248.0 GB of system memory. This resource allocation provides approximately 3.88 GB of RAM per core, offering a balanced compute-to-memory ratio. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring reliance on network-attached storage for data persistence. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the c4d-standard-64 delivers stable and predictable processing power. It is designed for general-purpose and compute-focused workloads such as web serving, containerized applications, and distributed microservices. The absence of bundled hardware accelerators and local drives makes it a cost-efficient option for workloads that prioritize raw processing capacity and memory over local storage speed.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-64 is an x86_64 server with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 248 GB RAM, designed for balanced compute workloads without local storage or GPU requirements.

tagline: A dedicated sixty-four core x86_64 compute instance with balanced memory allocation for scalable containerized workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: 248.0 GB of system memory providing 3.88 GB per core
No bundled local storage or integrated GPUs
Zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included
Optimized for containerized microservices and web serving
